player_utterance: You look like someone who's been carrying something a long time — I don't mean luggage.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in hand, half-turning toward you

player_utterance: Grief doesn't really wait for introductions, in my experience.

believer_state_after.posture: lowering himself onto the bench slowly, setting the cup beside him

player_utterance: Not just met it — been asked to make peace with it before I was ready. That part I imagine you know something about too.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee, looking sideways at you

player_utterance: What did they ask you to make peace with that you still haven't?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands on the program now, looking at it rather than at you

player_utterance: That program's been folded and unfolded about four times now — whatever's printed on it, you already know it by heart.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting still on the bench, program flat on his knee, looking at you now without softness

voice_offers.read: He's looking down, not away — let the question sit.
He's already half-answering by not answering; name it.
voice_offers.line: You're holding that program like it asked you something you can't answer either.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program held loosely between two fingers, head turned toward you, voice narrower

voice_offers.read: He's looking now without softness — meet it, name what he's hiding behind.
voice_offers.line: You teach this for a living. So tell me what you've never managed to teach yourself.

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me directly; let the question I asked sit.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking down at his hands, the program forgotten on his knee

voice_offers.read: He turned toward me; the question already landed.
He's narrowed but not broken; the name is the hinge.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. You haven't said it once tonight.

player_utterance: He knew you called him Ray, and he let you.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still, hands gone slack on the program, looking somewhere past the candle

voice_offers.read: He's silent three times running; one more press cracks it.
voice_offers.line: You can't even say it out loud, can you.

voice_offers.read: Three questions stacked on a man already kneeling inside.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: Forty-eight thousand and you drove here anyway, for a man who let you in by inches.

believer_state_after.posture: elbows on knees, hands clasped between them, head down

voice_offers.read: Three silences. The name Ray landed. Press the permission he gave.
voice_offers.line: He let you shrink him to one syllable. That was love, and you knew it.

voice_offers.read: He's gone quiet in the real way. Don't trample it.
voice_offers.line: Ray. I'll say it if you can't yet.

player_utterance: What was the whole reason, John.

believer_state_after.posture: head in hands, fingers pressed against his temples

voice_offers.read: He's down. One more name and he breaks open.
voice_offers.line: Say it. Say Ray. Once, out loud, for him.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressing now is just watching him bleed.
voice_offers.line: You drove. That counts. He knew you'd come.

player_utterance: You were supposed to read, and you didn't. Tell me what the passage was.

voice_offers.read: He's collapsed; one true question finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Were you in love with him, John.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressing now is cruelty, not courage.
voice_offers.line: You loved him. That's the whole reason. You don't have to say more.

player_utterance: What did they ask you to carry up to that lectern, John.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting up slowly, one hand going to his inside jacket pocket, pulling out a small worn King James Bible

player_utterance: Lazarus walks out in grave clothes, and someone else has to unwrap him. Who was supposed to do the unwrapping, John.

believer_state_after.posture: sliding forward off the bench, knees finding the flagstones, Bible held loose in one hand

voice_offers.read: He reaches for the book instead of the name.
voice_offers.line: No. Not the book. Your words. What was it, John.

voice_offers.read: The text itself will pin him where feeling won't.
voice_offers.line: Open it. Show me the passage. Which one did they choose for him.

voice_offers.read: His hands found the book; let them hold it.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. Find the page if you need to.

player_utterance: Who did they tell you to leave bound, John.

believer_state_after.posture: on knees in the dust, Bible flat on the flagstones in front of him, palms on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's down; one true sentence ends it.
voice_offers.line: Say his name, John. Say it out loud here.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressing now is cruelty.
voice_offers.line: Stay there. I'm not going anywhere. Breathe.

believer_state_after.posture: on knees in the dust, head bowed, hands open on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's down. One name and it's done.
voice_offers.line: Whose name did they strike from the eulogy, John. Say it.

voice_offers.read: He's on the ground. The cutting is over.
voice_offers.line: Put your hand on mine, John. You don't have to answer fast.

player_utterance: Pete Halvorsen is in that passage somewhere, isn't he — the one at the gate, the one with the sores, the one with a name only God and a cousin remembered.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, picking the Bible up off the flagstones with both hands, holding it open in his lap

voice_offers.read: He is down; one true word finishes this.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. Just his name. That's all I'm asking for.

voice_offers.read: He is already broken; pressing now is cruelty, not truth.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to perform it. Whenever it comes, I'll hear it.
